title: Farideh Doost Mohammadi
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Farideh Doost Mohammadi
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: I received the B.Sc. degree in electrical engineering from Iran University of Science and Technology, Tehran, in 2010, the M.Sc. degree (with first Hons.) in the Electrical Engineering Department, Amirkabir University of Technology, Tehran, in 2012, and the Ph.D. degree in the Electrical Engineering Department, West Virginia University, Morgantown, WV, USA, in 2017. My research interests include control systems, power system dynamic modeling, micro and smart grid, and renewable energy sources.
education_entries: Ph D in Electrical Engineering, West Virginia University University
MS in Electrical Engineering, Amirkabir University of Technology, Iran
BS in Electrical Engineering, Iran University of Science and Technology
research_interests: Power System Smart Grids Renewable Energies
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). A novel cooperative distributed secondary controller for VSI and PQ inverters of AC microgrids . Heliyon. Volume, 5. Issue, 6.
Conference Proceeding (2019). Power Management through Aging-Based Task Scheduling Algorithms for Smart Grids . 2019 Innovative Smart Grid Technologies NA.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2018). State-Space Modeling, Analysis, and Distributed Secondary Frequency Control of Isolated Microgrids . IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion. Volume, 33. Issue, 1. Pages, 155-165.
